mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate held near the zero lower bound (currently 0.12%) reduces the discount rate applied to equity cash flows and encourages capital rotation from cash/bonds into equities, producing a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as long as the rate remains near zero.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.12% indicates zero-lower-bound policy stance
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d SPX return falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX shows negative mean 20d returns during periods when FEDFUNDS stays below 0.25% for 3+ consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE has spiked above 8% (currently 10.0%) and then begins falling by at least 0.5 percentage points over a two-month window, this labor-market-recovery signal predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days because falling unemployment from crisis peaks historically coincides with reacceleration of earnings expectations.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls by more than 0.5pp over 2 months while prior level exceeds 8%
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 10.0% reflects a crisis-level peak consistent with a sharp downturn
regime_hint: post-shock labor market recovery from a UNRATE peak above 8%
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are not significantly higher (t-test p>0.05) during triggered UNRATE-decline episodes than in a matched non-triggered baseline sample, or Rank IC < 0.03 across rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of near-zero FEDFUNDS and a rebound in HOUST (housing starts) signals that ultra-loose monetary policy is successfully transmitting into interest-rate-sensitive housing demand, which historically leads broader consumption and equity market gains over a 60-trading-day horizon; the effect requires both conditions jointly, since low rates alone or housing rebound alone are weaker predictors.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 529K units alongside FEDFUNDS at 0.12% suggests early-stage rate-driven housing response
regime_hint: zero-rate monetary regime with recovering housing starts
failure_condition: The interaction term (HOUST momentum x low FEDFUNDS indicator) shows no incremental predictive power over either single-series model in walk-forward tests, or Rank IC of the joint signal < 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession recovery regime characterized by elevated UNRATE (>8%), a month-over-month increase in INDPRO signals resumption of industrial production capacity and predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days; the same INDPRO uptick carries weaker predictive power in mature expansion regimes where slack is already low.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 99.44 with UNRATE at 10.0% indicates industrial output recovering from a low base amid labor market slack
regime_hint: post-recession recovery regime with UNRATE above 8%
failure_condition: INDPRO MoM increases fail to precede positive 20d SPX returns in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE>8%, or Rank IC drops below 0.03 in that regime over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month surge in RSAFS (retail sales) exceeding 2% signals an unexpectedly rapid rebound in consumer demand, prompting a short-horizon positive repricing of cyclical equities within the next 5 trading days as markets digest the stronger-than-expected consumption data.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 352,073 million reflects a level consistent with a demand rebound phase
regime_hint: early-cycle demand rebound following a demand shock
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns following RSAFS surges above 2% are not significantly different from unconditional 5-day return distribution (p>0.05), or hit rate for positive returns falls below 50% across at least 20 triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS shows positive month-over-month job growth while UNRATE remains persistently elevated above 8%, this bifurcation indicates a fragile, uneven labor recovery that increases downside tail risk for equities over the next 20 trading days, since headline job gains mask structural slack that can cap consumer spending and corporate revenue growth.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 130,996K alongside UNRATE at 10.0% indicates a labor market still far below pre-shock employment levels despite payroll additions
regime_hint: uneven post-shock labor recovery with high structural unemployment
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns during PAYEMS-growth/high-UNRATE episodes are not significantly lower than baseline (p>0.05), or the interaction signal underperforms the single-series UNRATE model in walk-forward Rank IC comparisons.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero-interest-rate regime, an acceleration in CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth above the recent trend signals rising inflation risk that markets interpret as a precursor to eventual policy tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as forward discount rate expectations rise.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth exceeds trailing 6-month average by more than 0.3pp while FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 216.385 with FEDFUNDS at 0.12% reflects a low-rate, currently low-inflation baseline vulnerable to inflation surprise
regime_hint: zero-lower-bound monetary regime facing inflation risk repricing
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns following CPI acceleration triggers are not significantly negative (p>0.05) relative to baseline, or the signal's Rank IC is below 0.03 across rolling 2-year evaluation windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained quarter-over-quarter increase in real GDP (GDPC1) reflects broadening aggregate demand recovery and predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days, functioning as an always-on trend signal reflecting the trajectory of macroeconomic output independent of any specific threshold.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 12,990.3 billion chained dollars reflects the current level of real output relevant to trend assessment
failure_condition: Rank IC between GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth and forward 60d SPX return remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive GDPC1 growth quarters are followed by negative mean 60d SPX returns in more than 45% of cases.
